title: The Secret
subtitle: The bravest thing you can say is 'I don't know.'

dialogue.text: The roof. Drew's spot. You have to climb out the hallway window and step onto the slant — carefully. Drew showed Rush how last summer. Nobody else knows about it.
The sky is turning purple. Somewhere down the street, a dog barks. Normal sounds. Safe sounds.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: You know the best thing about up here?

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: The view?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Nobody can hear you yell.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: Are you going to yell?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Not tonight. Tonight's for talking.
They sit. Legs dangling over the edge, but not far enough to be dangerous. Drew would never let it be actually dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Can I tell you something? And you don't tell the girls.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: Yeah. Of course.
Drew is quiet for a long time. Long enough that the streetlights come on.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: I don't know what I'm supposed to be.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Like... everyone expects something. Be the responsible one. Be the leader. Make sure everyone's okay. But what if I don't know how? What if I'm just making it up?
This is Drew without the armor. Without the reassuring smile or the steady voice. Just a kid who's been carrying something heavier than he lets on.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: I'm scared I'm going to let you all down. Not in some big way. In the small ways. The ways you don't notice until it's too late.
The streetlight flickers. A car passes below. Normal world. Normal sounds. Extraordinary honesty.
Drew just told you the truest thing he has. What do you do with it?

choices.notification: Drew will remember that you were honest with him.

choices.text: "You're not going to let us down, Drew."
choices.notification: Drew will remember that you believed in him.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: ...I'm scared I'm not enough either.
Rush doesn't plan to say it. It just comes out. Like Drew's honesty made a space for it.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: Like... you're good at everything. And Lu's smart. And Yen is Yen. And I'm just... here.
Drew looks at Rush. Really looks. The way he did in the forest. Like he's deciding something.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Rush. Listen to me. You are not 'just here.' You're the one who shows up. Every time. When Lu needs someone to listen. When Yen is scared. When I need someone to carry the branches.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Showing up is the whole thing. Everything else is noise.
They sit there. Two brothers on a roof. Both scared. Both honest about it. And somehow, that makes it lighter.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: You're not going to let us down, Drew. You literally never have.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: ...Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: Yeah. You carried Yen home yesterday. You gave me your money at the market. You always take the worst sleeping spot. You never even complain.
Drew doesn't say anything for a moment. Just nods. And looks at the sky.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Thanks, Rush.
Two words. But Rush can hear everything inside them.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: Hey — did you see Lu's face when she swam today?
Drew pauses. Looks at Rush. Something closes behind his eyes. Not anger. Just... a door. He opened it, and Rush didn't walk through.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Yeah. She was great.
They talk about other things. Safe things. The moment passes. It won't come back.
A shape on the chimney. Green eyes catching starlight.
Kimiru is there. Of course he is. He's always there. But tonight, something is different.
He looks at Drew and Rush. Then he turns away. Faces the other direction. Gives them the sky.
Even the cat knows some things are private.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: We should go in. School tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: One more minute.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: ...One more minute.
They stay. The stars are out. The world is small and safe and theirs.
Somewhere below, Lu turns on a lamp. Yen is already asleep. The house is warm. And up on the roof, two brothers sit in the dark and don't need to say anything at all.
The bond between them isn't dramatic. It isn't tested by fire or proved by sacrifice. It's just this: showing up. Sitting on a roof. Telling the truth when it's easier not to.
Someday, Rush will sit on this roof alone. He doesn't know that yet. He can't.
For now, there are two.
